question: Question 2   Please select the best response that applies to a person living with dementia:
options: Option 1   People living with dementia are vulnerable
Option 2   People living with dementia have the same basic feeling (sad, glad, mad, scared)
Option 3  People living with dementia need love
Option 4. People living with dementia are independent and resilient
Option 5   People living with dementia are considered child like
Option 6   All of the above
Option 7   Only answers 1, 2, and 3 above

question: Question 5: The concept of Dementia Intelligence™, which includes the study of The Psychology of the Dementia Brain,  as introduced by the ICA, is furthered defined as:
options: Option 1 The need to always remember that those living with dementia have greatly diminished cognitive abilities and caregivers must meet them on their own perceptual level through the Psychology of the Dementia Brain (Adult Ego State and Child Ego State.)
Option 2   The recognition that those with dementia may also have very heightened sensibilities relating to emotional state, sensory inputs and even spiritual insights
Option 3  Realizing that dementia is not a static condition and that care providers must continually adapt their communications to connect with the person living with dementia.
Option 4  All of the above

question: Question 6: Under the TDI Model, the following staffing changes are integral to the success of the program:
options: Option 1 CNAs are required to work only 6-hour shifts to avoid burnout but are paid for 8 hours
Option 2 A Dementia Coach must be on site and on duty, or available (on call).
Option 3   The TDI system creates a new position for the organization, the Dementia Coach and CNAs are required to work a 6-hour shift
Option 4   Family members can choose to become a TDI care partner and become an important part of the care team.
Option 5  Training, demonstrating, coaching and advising management become a daily responsibility of the Dementia Coach in the TDI system.
Option 6  All of the above

question: Question 10: Touch is very important for someone who welcomes touching. Not everyone living with dementia will accept touching. Please select the touches that are most often practiced in the course of providing care to a person living with dementia.
options: Option 1  Hand massage with lotion
Option 2  Brush or comb their hair
Option 3  Give the person a manicure
Option 4   All of the above

question: Question 11: There are two other major themes, and two minor themes, of engagement in the fieldwork article entitled Definitions and Measurement of Engagement in Persons with Dementia by William Matcham, et al (2022). These served to capture affect, attitude, and attention of the participant. What were the two major themes?
options: Option 1:  Playing table top games
Option 2:  Social contact/interaction
Option 3: Response to activity and/or stimuli
Option 4:  Meaningful/satisfying activity
Option 5:  All of the above
Option 6:  Both option 2 and 3

question: Question 16: If word choices offer the least impact in communication, what inferences could we draw from that in the field of dementia caregiving?
options: Option 1  Care should focus on their posture towards those they serve
Option 2. Care providers should be very attentive to their tone of voice
Option 3. Care providers should not talk about memory dependent topics
Option 4   All of the above

question: Question 17: In total, how many personalities can an individual display at anytime (please include effective and ineffective personalities)?
options: Option 1 - Three
Option 2 - Five
Option 3 - Six

question: Question 18: What do we mean by symbiosis in The Psychology of the Dementia Brain in the TDI Model.
options: Option 1  Symbiosis has to do with group singing
Option 2  Symbiosis means simply shifting between ego states

Option 3 Symbiosis describes a relationship of dependence between the person living with dementia and their care provider (i.e., making decisions for the other’s wellbeing even though they are an adult--assuming the responsibility of their Parent Ego State for their safety, wellbeing, and care.) At some point in their dementia journey, only the Child Ego state remains intact and reliable.
Option 4. All of the above.
Option 5  None of the above

question: Question 19: Why is it vital for the Dementia Coach to know all about symbiosis with the ability to relate and teach others on the care team whether at home, in adult day care, assisted living or skilled nursing?
options: Option 1. Symbiotic relationship occurs when individuals only function with a limited number of ego states.
Option 2.  Mastery of a symbiotic relationship by care staff is vital to effective dementia care.
Option 3  Symbiotic relationships can lower stress and provide feelings of safety in persons living with dementia.
Option 4. All of the above
Option 5. None of the above

question: Question 20: Calling the person living with dementia by their first name brings them into the present--and adding "listen to me" captures their active attention. (Adult Ego State). What additional two tips does the short film (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-XFm5pVL-zg), recommend to do?
options: Option 1 Rub their neck softly with a warm cloth
Option 2. Make eye contact and then introduce yourself
Option 3. Use short, clear sentences
Option  4  All of the above
Option 5  Options 2 and 3
Option 6  None of the above

question: Question 21: What happens when a care provider poses a question to the person living with dementia that requires thinking and/or reasoning?
options: Option 1 It invites the person to become agitated.
Option 2 It reminds the person that they have memory loss
Option 3.It forces the person living with dementia to become quiet and unresponsive.
Option 4  It creates an immediate disconnect emotionally.
Option 5.  All of the above.
Option 6  None of the above

question: BONUS Question 1: Why is the book TA TODAY: A New Introduction to Transactional Analysis by Ian Stewart and Vann Jones so relevant to successful dementia care?
options: Option 1. Because those living with dementia may be suffering with a number of comorbidities
Option 2. Because learning games such as dealmaking and negotiating are particularly stimulating for those with dementia
Option 3. Because the person living with dementia functions with a limited number of ego states.
Option 4. Because a person living with dementia functions largely with their Child ego states while both the Parent and Adult ego states are most often unreliable.
Option 5.  Because being able to apply TA offers tremendous adaptive power to effectively communicate with those with dementia
Option 6.  All of the above
Option 7   Answers 3-5 only
Option 8   None of the above

question: BONUS Question 2: What is Functional Symbiosis and how does it work?
options: Option 1.  Functional Symbiosis describes a relationship where 2 people behave as though they are one.

Option 2.  The care provider primarily uses their Parent and Adult ego states (i.e., the care provider is seen as the provider or authority figure, while the person living with dementia is seen as dependent and needing guidance.)
Option 3. The care provider assuming a symbiosis provides guidance, safety, directives (brushing teeth, dentist appointments, showers, etc.)
Option 4. All of the above
Option 5.  Answers 1 & 3 above
Option 6.  None of the above
